hook.text: Every morning, the team stands in a circle for exactly 15 minutes. Three questions. That's it.

buildup.text: What did you do yesterday? What will you do today? What's blocking you?

discovery.text: Standing keeps it short. The time box forces focus. Problems surface immediately.

twist.text: Many teams turn standups into status reports for managers — the exact opposite of the intent.

climax.text: Done right, the standup is the team's daily pulse check — fast, honest, and action-oriented.

punchline.text: Fifteen minutes of honesty beat an hour of reporting.

quiz.question: Why do teams stand during the daily standup?
options: It improves blood flow for creativity
